items.title: Properly regulated sex offender registry can boost public safety, says criminologist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia can significantly boost public safety without infringing on human rights by establishing a properly regulated sex offender registry, a criminologist said. He said Malaysia can easily tailor its own registry to suit local needs rather than duplicating foreign models wholesale. Sundramoorthy said registries are preventive risk-management tools designed for harm reduction, rather than public humiliation.

items.title: Quiet Chinese county hit by deadly coal mine disaster
items.summary: COAL miners in the sleepy Chinese county of Qinyuan sometimes dine at Zhang's skewer eatery, especially on payday, so a gas explosion that killed at least 82 of these workers left her feeling sorrow for their bereaved families. The tragedy unfolded in northern Shanxi province, with preliminary findings showing the company operating the mine had committed "serious" violations, state media reported Saturday. The blast caused China's worst mining disaster in 17 years, with search efforts ongoing to find two people still missing, state broadcaster CCTV reported.

items.title: Fahmi wants talks with TikTok to assist media to monetise livestreams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry plans to hold discussions with TikTok on enabling Malaysian media organisations to monetise their livestream content. He said media outlets should be able to generate revenue from audiences drawn to their official platforms. Speaking to reporters later, Fahmi said the concern extended beyond TikTok, as many social media platforms benefited from content produced by media organisations and official media accounts.

items.title: Only 10pct of Kedah padi farmers begin dry-season planting
items.summary: Malaysian Padi Farmers Association (PeSAWAH) chairman Abdul Rashid Yob said the situation arose as farmers faced a dilemma between the risk of water shortages during the dry season and the threat of flooding if rain continues. "Although rain is still falling at the moment, water levels at the dams — particularly Muda Dam — have not yet stabilised enough to allow large-scale water releases for irrigation purposes. There is water in the padi fields because of the rain, but dam water levels are still not high enough.

items.title: Pas holds emergency meeting over ties with Bersatu in PN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas held an emergency meeting today to discuss issues concerning Perikatan Nasional (PN), including the party's future with its component partner, Bersatu. It is learnt that the meeting, chaired by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang in Kuala Terengganu, involved the party's top leadership, including the Pas Political Bureau and Central Committee. Sources said among the Pas leaders in attendance were deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man, secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, and vice-president Datuk Idris Ahmad.

items.title: Kampung Tigot bridge in Sabah reopens after year‑long closure, restoring vital rural access
items.summary: KENINGAU, May 24 — The reopening of the Kampung Tigot bridge after nearly a year of closure has restored a crucial transport link for several rural villages that rely heavily on the route for daily commuting, healthcare, education and economic activities. Kampung Tigot Village Development and Security Committee (JKKK) chairman Basilius Donald said the closure of the bridge had posed significant challenges to residents, who were forced to use alternative routes for almost a year. The damaged old wooden bridge.

items.title: Body of 11-year-old boy reported missing near NKVE found at Sungai Buloh riverbank
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — An 11-year-old boy reported missing near a river by the NKVE close to Menara Finas in Damansara Damai, Petaling Jaya, was found dead this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the boy’s body was found by the riverbank at Jalan Welfare, Kampung Baru Sungai Buloh. The search resumed this morning along the riverbank from Paya Jaras Hilir Bridge to Merbau Sempak Bridge, before the boy was found.

items.title: Two brothers feared drowned while swimming in Sungai Paka
items.summary: The victims were identified as Mohamad Amir Ashraf Mohd Zulnaidi, 15, and Mohd Aidil Amsar Mohd Zulnaidi, 14, students of SMKA Durian Guling, Marang and SMK Paka, Dungun respectively. According to an initial report from the Fire and Rescue Department, an emergency call was received at 11.19am before fire engines from the Paka Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene six minutes later. The two survivors were identified as Mohd Farish Iqbal Cik Mohana Runasdi, 14, from SMK Paka, and Mohd Afi Naashif Adli Noone, 15.

items.title: Nilai police arrest three men, woman over robbery and snatch‑theft cases in Tangkak
items.summary: NILAI, May 24 — Police detained three men and a woman suspected of involvement in robbery and snatch theft cases in Tangkak, Johor, during an operation at Dataran Nilai, Putra Point here yesterday. In a statement, Nilai police chief Supt Johari Yahya said the local suspects, aged between 22 and 28, attempted to flee, resulting in a struggle before they were apprehended. Yesterday, a post on social media platform Threads alleged a shootout between police and criminals at Dataran Nilai, claiming the suspects’ vehicle windows were damaged by gunfire.

items.title: Johor PKR slams ex-leaders, members over immature political behaviour
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Johor PKR has condemned the behaviour of former members and leaders for what it described as politically immature conduct, saying they continue to hurl baseless accusations without any factual basis after leaving the party. Without naming specific individuals, Johor PKR leadership council chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa said these former leaders and members appeared to favour using media platforms to spread perceptions that risked confusing all parties, including PKR members. "The fact is, Johor PKR continues to move forward with a strong leadership structure, an active machinery and grassroots members who remain loyal to the reform struggle," she said in a statement today.

items.title: Nissan unit scraps plan to make EV powertrains in UK
items.summary: TOKYO: Nissan Motor subsidiary JATCO has scrapped its plan to make electric vehicle (EV) powertrains in Sunderland, Britain, in light of sluggish demand for Nissan's EVs in Europe, the Nikkei business daily reported on Sunday. In January 2025, JATCO announced a plan to invest 48.7 million pounds (US$65.39 million) to make up to 340,000 units a year of EV powertrains that integrate the motor, inverter and reducer at a Sunderland plant for Nissan. No one was immediately available for comment at Nissan outside regular business hours, and a query submitted via JATCO's website has yet to receive a response.

items.title: Penang Sukma rugby player dies after collapsing during training
items.summary: KULIM: A Penang Sukma rugby player died after collapsing during a training session at the General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 2 football field here yesterday. Kulim police chief Deputy Superintendent Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said Muhammad Adam Putra Syahril, 21, collapsed and lost consciousness while training was underway about 5.30pm. Tengku Mohd Faisal said the student of a higher learning institution in Perlis was given initial assistance by his coach before being rushed by ambulance to Kulim Hospital, where he was pronounced dead by a medical officer at the emergency unit.

items.title: Inflation storm sweeps across Mekong region, pricey basic necessities hit consumers
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Millions of consumers across the Mekong region are suffering from the rising cost of living as galloping inflation bites into their daily incomes. Inflation is puncturing economic growth stories. Laos faces the brunt with a 10.2 per cent inflation rate, Vietnam at 5.46 per cent, Cambodia's 5.79 and Thailand at 2.89 per cent.

items.title: World facing 'unpredictable anarchic disorder', says economist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The world today is not experiencing a coherent "new world order" but what could be described as an "unpredictable anarchic disorder" marked by militarisation, weakening multilateralism and widening inequality, said prominent economist Jomo Kwame Sundaram. Speaking at a public lecture titled "What is at Stake in a Fragmented World Today: Rethinking Peace, Imperialism, and the Global Disorder" at the International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM) here recently, he said what is often described as a "new world order" was neither entirely new nor fully reflective of a structured global order. It is becoming a kind of unpredictable anarchic disorder," he said.

items.title: From seaweed to Sabah Tea, state eyes stronger IP push for local products
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 24 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN), through the Intellectual Property Corporation of Malaysia (MyIPO), is developing a comprehensive intellectual property protection mechanism in the state through the Sabah Intellectual Property Development Plan 2026-2030. According to Armizan, the plan will focus on developing a more integrated intellectual property ecosystem, including identifying new Geographical Indications (GIs) for high-value products such as highland agricultural produce, seaweed, and local handicrafts. The achievement places Sabah among the main contributors to the country’s GI portfolio through renowned products such as Sabah Tea, Tenom Coffee, Borneo Virgin Coconut Oil, Sabah Seaweed, Tawau Cocoa, Tambunan Ginger and Sabah Batik.

items.title: Woman assaulted by husband after refusing to give him RM30 for drugs
items.summary: Saratok police chief Deputy Superintendent Mathew Manggie said the 26-year-old man was arrested following a report lodged by his wife. He said the suspect became aggressive after the victim refused to give him the RM30 he had requested to purchase drugs. "Following the report received, police launched an investigation and arrested the suspect, who later surrendered himself," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Man suspected of stabbing woman in neck near Alor Setar remanded four more days
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 24 — A 32-year-old man suspected of stabbing a woman he knew in the neck inside a car in Titi Gajah, Kepala Batas, near here, has reportedly been remanded for another four days. According to a report in Berita Harian , Magistrate Nur Syifa Mohd Hamzah issued the extension order at the Alor Setar Magistrates’ Court today, after the suspect’s initial seven-day remand expired. Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali reportedly said the remand had been extended until Wednesday to assist investigations.

items.title: Minimum social media age of 16 a reasonable preventive step, says lawyer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The proposed minimum age of 16 years for opening social media accounts is seen as a preventive measure with reasonable justification based on the principle of duty of care. She said Malaysia already has a comprehensive legal framework, including the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, and the Evidence Act 1950, covering digital evidence aspects. "This also involves cross-border cooperation as well as the speed of digital platforms in responding to harmful content," she said.

items.title: Man searches for wife missing after grocery outing
items.summary: MELAKA: A man is pleading for the return of his wife who went missing after leaving home to buy groceries at a supermarket in Taman Malim Jaya last Thursday. Her husband, Mohamad Farouk Abdul Salam, 49, said he only realised she was missing after trying to contact her when he arrived home. He said a police report was lodged at Malim police station last Friday out of concern for his wife's safety.

items.title: Driver to be charged over fatal Kuala Terengganu crash that killed youth
items.summary: Terengganu police chief Datuk Mohd Khairi Khairudin said the 43-year-old self-employed man would be charged with reckless and dangerous driving. He said police had recorded the suspect's statement shortly after the accident. "Once completed, he will be charged under Section 41 of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving," Khairi said.

items.title: Missing boy, 11, found drowned after overnight search in Sungai Buloh
items.summary: SUNGAI BULOH: An 11-year-old boy who was reported missing after falling into a river near Damansara Damai was found drowned this morning following an overnight search operation. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the victim was found at 10.47am by the department's Water Rescue Team (PPDA) near Jalan Welfare, Kampung Baru Sungai Buloh. "Personnel from the Sungai Buloh Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by a Water Rescue Team from the Shah Alam Fire and Rescue Station, were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Vintage pics show Gaza 'we no longer know'
items.summary: WHEN Kegham Djeghalian photographed daily life in Gaza last century, the Palestinian territory was synonymous with Hollywood-inspired brides, fancy dress parties and excursions to smoke a hookah at the beach. "It's a Gaza we no longer know. Djeghalian survived the Armenian genocide of 1915 – a term strongly denied by Turkiye – then settled in Gaza, opening the city's first ever photo studio in 1944.

items.title: Soldier charged with kicking son's stomach, slapping his face
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A soldier was charged at the Sessions Court here today with injuring his 10-year-old son in Kampung Kijang near here on May 11 last year. According to the charge, the man was accused of abusing the boy by kicking the child's stomach and slapping his face, which may have caused physical injury and emotional distress. Deputy public prosecutor Nur Kamilah Che Ibrahim prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Samsul Bahrin.

items.title: Trump: Gunman had 'violent history and possible obsession' with White House
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the gunman who opened fire on Secret Service officers outside the White House had a "violent history and possible obsession" with the building. "Thank you to our great Secret Service and Law Enforcement for the swift and professional action taken this evening against a gunman near the White House, who had a violent history and possible obsession with our Country's most cherished structure," he said in a post on Truth Social.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: 29 undocumented migrants charged with immigration offences in Pasir Mas
items.summary: PASIR MAS: Seven women were among 29 undocumented migrants charged at the Sessions Court here today with various immigration offences. Six were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for overstaying. Two migrants were also charged under Section 56(1)(l) of the Immigration Act for possessing fake immigration stamps.

items.title: Soldier pleads not guilty to abusing son in Kota Bharu
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 24 — A soldier pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing his son two years ago. Zainudin Zainal Abidin, 42, made the plea after the charge was read out before Judge Zulkifli Abllah. — Bernama * If you suspect child abuse, call the following hotlines for free and confidential support: Talian Kasih at 15999 or WhatsApp 019-2615999 (24/7); Talian BuddyBear at 1800-18-2327(BEAR) (noon-midnight daily); and One Crisis Centre (24/7) Wilayah Persekutuan at 03-26155555 (Kuala Lumpur General Hospital), 03-61454333 (Sungai Buloh Hospital) or 03-83124200 (Putrajaya Hospital).

items.title: Victims in online sexual harassment cases urged to prioritise safety, preserve digital evidence, says former Selangor Bar chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Online sexual harassment is becoming an increasingly serious concern, with victims urged to act quickly to preserve digital evidence as crucial material can easily be deleted or altered, said former Selangor Bar chairman Kokila Vaani Vadiveloo. She said Malaysia’s Anti-Sexual Harassment Act 2022 defines sexual harassment as any unwanted conduct of a sexual nature, whether verbal, non-verbal, visual, gestural or physical, with the legal framework increasingly recognising that harassment can also occur in digital spaces. “Online harassment is very much recognised under Malaysian law.

items.title: MOE probing alleged sexual misconduct involving Setiu teacher
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Education Ministry is investigating an alleged sexual misconduct involving a teacher at a school in Setiu that went viral on social media. The Terengganu Education Department said investigations are ongoing and urged the public to channel any information related to the case to the department. "The department has zero tolerance for sexual misconduct in its educational institutions and will take action against anyone found guilty," the department said in a statement today.

items.title: Welfare Dept records eightfold rise in autism PwD registrations since 2015
items.summary: MELAKA, May 24 — The number of registered persons with disabilities (PwDs) with autism under the Social Welfare Department (JKM) has increased eightfold to 90,459 as of April 30, compared with 2015. JKM director-general Datuk Che Murad Sayang Ramjan said the increase is believed to be due to the effectiveness of autism-related awareness programmes and campaigns among parents, actively carried out by the government through cooperation with various parties, including state governments and the National Autism Society of Malaysia (NASOM) “It is possible that in 2015, many children were already living with autism, but many people still did not understand or were unaware of what autism was until awareness increased following the implementation of various programmes. Che Murad Sayang said the facility was established to help ease the burden on parents in obtaining suitable treatment or therapy for their children.

items.title: Sarawak ready to support Trans-Borneo railway project: Abang Johari
items.summary: KUCHING: The Sarawak state government is ready to support the proposed Trans-Borneo Railway project to strengthen transportation connectivity and improve logistics efficiency in Sabah and Sarawak. Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said the state already has a preliminary alignment for the project, which will be refined and agreed upon with the Federal Government before implementation begins. The proposed Trans-Borneo Railway project will connect Sarawak, Sabah, Brunei and Kalimantan, Indonesia.

items.title: Shein to buy apparel retailer Everlane
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: US apparel brand Everlane said on Friday it had reached an agreement to be acquired by online retailer Shein, giving the fast-fashion company ownership of a brand known for sustainability and supply-chain transparency. Everlane will remain independent, its chief executive officer Alfred Chang said in a statement, adding that the brand will hold its sustainability commitments while expanding to a global reach through the deal. Shein has long admired Everlane and plans to use the brand to enhance its own image of just affordable fast-fashion and drive cross-selling opportunities, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters, adding that there were multiple bidders for Everlane.

items.title: EU must reform, consolidate, use joint debt to cope with spending needs
items.summary: NICOSIA: European Union countries will face large bills for defence, energy and pensions in the next 15 years, the International Monetary Fund told EU finance ministers on Saturday, suggesting a mix of reforms, consolidation and joint borrowing as a way to manage that. Pension reforms and a higher retirement age would also help, as would government guarantees for riskier investments in low-carbon and climate-resilient projects that would help attract private capital to them. The IMF said that even with reforms, most EU countries would still need fiscal consolidation to put debt on a declining path, though the more ambitious the reforms, the less consolidation would be needed.

items.title: RTD seizes national footballer's Toyota Vellfire over expired road tax, insurance
items.summary: ALOR STAR: A luxury multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) belonging to a national footballer was seized during a luxury vehicle special operation on Thursday. Kedah Road Transport Department (RTD) director Stien Van Lutam said the Toyota Vellfire was driven by the footballer's brother-in-law and was stopped in front of the Alor Star Prison about 11.30am. Stien said the special operation was carried out by the department's Motorcycle Enforcement Unit to ensure all vehicle owners comply with road regulations and laws, regardless of status or background.

items.title: PKR Youth demands explanation from MPs linked to Bersama event
items.summary: PADANG RENGAS: PKR Youth has urged members of parliament seen at an event involving Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) to clarify their involvement, saying party members are entitled to seek explanations over the matter. He said it was reasonable for PKR grassroots members to raise concerns over the matter, particularly following recent political developments involving former party leaders. Kamil also expressed confidence that the party leadership would seek explanations from those involved.

items.title: Police: Viral clip shows arrest of four people, no shooting occurred in Nilai
items.summary: NILAI: Police have denied claims that a shooting incident occurred on a road near Dataran Nilai here last night, saying that a clip which went viral on social media is actually related to the arrest of four people. District police chief Superintendent Johari Yahya said that in the incident about 10pm, four suspects, including two siblings, were detained for their suspected involvement in a robbery case in Johor. "This morning, the Nilai district police headquarters detected a viral video on social media showing an arrest operation by police at Dataran Nilai, Putra Point, here.

items.title: Stampin MP urges review after Kuching micro‑businessman faces cash‑flow crisis over RM110 scam‑linked account closures
items.summary: Stampin MP Chong Chieng Jen said the victim, identified only as Liaw, had been running a micro food business for two years and also took online orders. “He also takes orders online. “However, last month, another bank informed him about the closure of his account,” Chong said in a statement.

items.title: Minister proposes gazetting Dayak miring as National Heritage, paving way for Unesco bid
items.summary: SIBU, May 24 — The National Unity Ministry has proposed that the traditional Dayak ceremony, namely ‘miring’, be gazetted as National Heritage. Therefore, he said the Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (MOTAC), through the National Heritage Department, needs to gazette the ceremony as a national heritage first so that it can be documented, preserved and promoted more comprehensively. The ceremony, held to welcome the upcoming Gawai festival, was jointly organised by Sarakup Indu Dayak Sarawak (SIDS) Sibu, Sarawak Dayak National Union (SDNU) Sibu and Orang Ulu National Association (OUNA) Sibu.

items.title: Police: Man found dead in Proton Wira in Baling had 20 criminal, drug records
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — A man found dead inside a locked car near a Chinese cemetery in Simpang Tiga Tawar, Baling, yesterday had 20 previous criminal and narcotics records, police reportedly said. According to Sinar Harian , Baling police chief Superintendent Brandon Anak Richard Joe said the man was a Malaysian in his 30s. Police have reportedly seized the car for further investigations.

items.title: Man arrested for stabbing woman inside car remanded four more days
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Police have obtained a remand extension for a 32-year-old man arrested for allegedly stabbing a woman inside her car at a restaurant here last Sunday. Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali said the suspect, whose initial seven-day remand order expires today, will now be remanded until Wednesday to assist in investigations. He said the extension was issued by magistrate Nur Syifa Mohd Hamzah at the Alor Star Court Complex here today.

items.title: MOE probing alleged sexual misconduct involving teacher in Setiu
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, May 24 — An alleged sexual misconduct case involving a teacher at a school in Setiu, which went viral on social media, is being investigated by the Integrity Unit of the Ministry of Education (MOE). The Terengganu Education Department (JPNT) said that investigations are ongoing and the public is urged to channel any information to the department. Action will be taken against anyone found guilty,” the department said in a statement.

items.title: CPO futures to trade with bearish bias next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Crude palm oil (CPO) futures on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives are expected to trade with a bearish bias next week amid weakness in the crude oil market, said a trader. "We expect prices to trade between RM4,350 and RM4,550 per tonne next week," he told Bernama. Interband Group of Companies senior palm oil trader Jim Teh said next week will be a short trading week due to the public holidays.

items.title: China's JD.com weighs £2bil bid for UK retailer The Very Group
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chinese e-commerce giant JD.com is evaluating a potential £2 billion (US$2.69 billion) bid for the British online shopping platform The Very Group, Sky News reported on Friday, in its latest push to expand in the UK market. The move would follow JD.com's earlier UK efforts, including its failed takeover bid for electricals group Currys and its decision last year to walk away from talks to acquire Argos from Sainsbury's. Carlyle took control of the group last year, ending the Barclay family's long-standing involvement in the business.

items.title: ‘We need the president’: Perak PKR urges Anwar to defend Tambun in next general election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Perak PKR has reportedly asked Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to defend Tambun in the next general election, saying his presence remains important to the party’s campaign in the state. However, Perak PKR chairman Mohamad Hairul Amir Sabri said there had been no discussion so far on whether Anwar would switch seats, according to a report in The Star . “We in Perak very much need the president to remain in Perak to help us win more parliamentary and state seats in the next election,” he was quoted as saying.

items.title: Ewon: RM16.8m Timpangoh–Babagon Bridge completed over three months ahead of schedule
items.summary: PENAMPANG, May 24 — The RM16.8 million Timpangoh-Babagon Bridge project has been completed more than three months ahead of schedule, providing a major boost to economic activities and connectivity for residents in Mukim Babagon. He said the completion of the bridge would significantly improve economic activities in the area, particularly for agricultural communities. In another development, Ewon said SMK Kipovo in Kampung Babagon is expected to begin operations next year.

items.title: From postal hub to cultural space: The story of KL’s old General Post Office
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Dataran Merdeka boasts many historical elements, but one of the most eye-catching architectural buildings is the old General Post Office (GPO), which evokes a deep history in Malaysia’s postal system. Situated in the heart of Kuala Lumpur’s heritage area, the GPO stands strong at the intersection of Jalan Hishamuddin and Jalan Raja, just a stone’s throw away from the iconic Sultan Abdul Samad Building. Opened in 1907, the GPO is one of the most spectacular post offices ever built in Malaysia.

items.title: Driver pinned in cab, fractures leg in two‑lorry collision near Bau
items.summary: KUCHING, May 24 — A lorry driver sustained a fractured left leg after two lorries collided at Kampung Buso along Jalan Bau-Kuching yesterday afternoon. “The incident involved two lorries that collided. “The driver of the other lorry was uninjured,” Bomba said in a statement.

items.title: Man found dead after collapsing from chair at Sarikei open‑air market, police rule out foul play
items.summary: SARIKEI. May 24 — A man was found dead at a food premises at the open air market along Jalan Merdeka here on Friday after collapsing from his chair. Sarikei District Police Chief Supt Aswandy Anis said initial investigations found that the man was earlier believed to have fallen asleep at one of the tables at the food premises at about 4am.

items.title: PM hails national regu team after Malaysia win 2026 Sepak Takraw World Cup
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim congratulated Malaysia’s national team regu for winning the 2026 Sepak Takraw World Cup at Stadium Titiwangsa last night. Anwar, in a Facebook post today, said the achievement not only made the country’s sports scene proud but also proved that strong fighting spirit, discipline, and unity can elevate Malaysia onto the world stage. “Congratulations to our national heroes — continue striving for excellence for our beloved Malaysia!” he said.

items.title: Pakatan Penang establishes manifesto, information and mobilisation committees for GE16
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 24 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) Penang has set up three new committees in preparation for the 16th General Election (GE16) as an early move to strengthen the coalition’s readiness at the state level. Penang PH chairman Chow Kon Yeow said the three committees are the Election Manifesto Committee led by Zairil Khir Johari from DAP, the Information Committee headed by Lee Boon Heng (PKR) and the Mobilisation Committee chaired by Zahar Zainul (Amanah). He said the line-up also includes three vice-chairmen, namely Ramkarpal Singh (DAP), Dr Zaidi Zakaria (Amanah) and Goh Choon Aik (PKR), while Lim Hui Ying (DAP) was appointed secretary and Zulkiefly Saad (PKR) assistant secretary.

items.title: Guan Eng urges Anwar to reverse 50pc Bumiputera equity rule for GLC, GLIC property acquisitions above RM20m
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — DAP veteran Lim Guan Eng today urged Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to roll back a new requirement for buyers of GLC and GLIC properties worth RM20 million and above to have at least 50 per cent Bumiputera equity. In a press statement, Lim said the updated Ministry of Economy Properties Acquisition Guidelines (PAG), which came into effect on November 18 last year, should revert to the previous 30 per cent threshold. “The increase in bumi equity ownership [requirement] from 30 per cent to 50 per cent by the current government has not only caused disillusionment amongst the PH base supporters but also raises concerns that this government may do the same for other policy instruments and administrative arrangements,” he said.

items.title: Queen of Malaysia arrives in Turkiye for three‑day special visit, to attend RMN ship‑launch ceremony
items.summary: ISTANBUL, May 24 — Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, has arrived in Istanbul, Turkiye in conjunction with a three-day special visit to the country. Her Majesty was accompanied by the Tunku Temenggong of Johor Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim, Tunku Panglima of Johor Tunku Abdul Rahman Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim and Tunku Putera of Johor Tunku Abu Bakar Al-Haj Ibni Sultan Ibrahim. Also present were Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, Malaysia’s Consul General in Istanbul Ahmad Amiri Abu Bakar, Istana Negara Grand Chamberlain Datuk Azuan Effendy Zairakithnaini and Comptroller of the Royal Household of Johor Colonel (Rtd) Datuk Mohamed Perang Musa.

items.title: Police: Man who drove against traffic in KL tests positive for drugs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Police arrested a man in his 30s for driving against traffic along Jalan Hang Tuah here yesterday afternoon. Cheras district police chief ACP Rosdi Daud said the man also tested positive for amphetamine dan methamphetamine. A video depicting a vehicle being driven against traffic and then across a road divider went viral earlier.

items.title: No restrictions yet on non‑Islamic houses of worship in commercial areas, says Selangor exco
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 24 — The Selangor State Government has not enforced nor implemented planning guidelines that bar non-Islamic houses of worship from being built in commercial areas, said State Executive Councillor (Exco) for Investment, Trade and Mobility Ng Sze Han. Ng, who is also the state Special Committee For Non-Islamic Affairs (LIMAS) Co-Chairman, said the review of said guidelines will be carried out by the state government in consultation with religious associations, with a meeting on the matter to be arranged early next month. “The state government is dedicated to resolving this administrative and technical alignment through constructive dialogue, ensuring that Selangor remains a progressive, fair, and inclusive state for all,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Ipoh police hunt two brothers over theft of cash box from ‘budget rice’ seller
items.summary: Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the first individual identified is Mohd Faiz Naharoddin, 36, also known as ‘Paeh’. “He is estimated to be about 180 centimetres tall, has a medium complexion and a slim build, with his last known address at Halaman Meru Damai, Ipoh. “The second individual is his younger brother, Mohamad Arif Naharoddin, 25, also known as ‘Aboy’, who shares the same last known address in Halaman Meru Damai,” he said in a statement tonight.

items.title: Teenage girl reported missing while hiking in Tanjong Malim, search underway
items.summary: IPOH, May 24 — A teenage girl has been reported missing after possibly being lost while hiking at Bukit Changkat Asa near Tanjong Malim yesterday. Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said they received a call at 1.26 pm and dispatched a team of firefighters to the location. The search and rescue operation was suspended at 7 pm yesterday and would be handed over to the Kuala Kubu Bharu Fire and Rescue Department for further action, he added.

items.title: MB: Johor declares Monday special holiday after JDT win Malaysia Cup
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 24 — The Johor state government has declared a special state holiday on Monday following Johor Darul Ta’zim’s (JDT) Malaysia Cup win last night. Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the declaration was made with the consent of Johor Regent, Tunku Mahkota Ismail in appreciation to the Johor people and Southern Tiger fans for their undivided support to the team. The success proved the continued commitment of the Johor leadership in developing modern high performance football that made JDT the state’s pride and a respected team at the national and Asian level, he added.

items.title: King congratulates JDT on historic fourth straight Malaysia Cup win
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has extended his congratulations to the Johor Darul Ta’zim Football Club (JDT) on their success in capturing their sixth Malaysia Cup title yesterday. According to a post on the Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar Facebook page, His Majesty also expressed his appreciation for the fighting spirit shown by the players and coaching staff, as well as the unwavering support of the loyal JDT fans who stand by the team through thick and thin. Sultan Ibrahim also extended his congratulations to his son, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Regent of Johor, who is also the owner of JDT.

items.title: Anwar takes over Pandan as adopted constituency after Rafizi’s exit, Tengku Zafrul named monitoring officer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) president, has taken over Pandan as his adopted parliamentary constituency following the departure of its former MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli from the party. PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said Prime Minister’s senior political adviser Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz had meanwhile been appointed as the party’s monitoring officer for the constituency. “Tengku Zafrul has also been entrusted to monitor the Ampang parliamentary constituency.

items.title: Police declare bomb threat at prominent KL building a hoax after sweep, probe underway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Police have confirmed that a bomb threat made against a prominent building in the capital last night was false. Brickfields district police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said police received a call regarding the incident at 9.09 pm from a security guard at the building. “The building’s security guard informed us that he had received an email sent to the customer service centre stating that an individual had placed a bomb on the 23rd floor of the building’s north tower,” he said in a statement.

items.title: One shock away from crisis: Report says nearly a fifth of PPR households have no savings, over half can last only three months or less
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 24 — Public housing in Malaysia, originally designed as short-term shelter for the urban poor, now risks keeping its tenants for far longer than intended, according to a recent Think City study. The report found that almost three-quarters of some 3,000 People’s Housing Project (PPR) residents surveyed are vulnerable to economic shocks. The report, titled “From Roof to Resilience”, polled 2,884 people across 10 public housing communities in the Klang Valley.
